reasoning: Recent polling from April 2025 clearly indicates that the political landscape in Canada has drastically shifted following Mark Carney becoming leader of the Liberal Party. Entering the campaign (which is currently underway), the Liberals overtook the Conservatives. As of mid-April, pollsters including Abacus, Nanos, and Mainstreet consistently show the Liberals leading the popular vote by 3 to 5 points (roughly 43% to 38/39%). For the Conservative Party to win the popular vote by specifically a 9% to 12% margin, they would need a 12 to 17 point swing in their favor during the final few weeks of the campaign. Such a massive late swing is extremely rare historically. Furthermore, even if the Conservatives did mount a comeback, their margin of victory would need to land squarely within this narrow 3-point band. Given the combination of their current popular vote deficit, the short amount of time remaining until the election, and the precise bracket required, the probability is heavily restricted and sits at the 0.02 floor.
